source_note: Paraphrased starting templates based on the Spectrum 5.1 framework structure (UK Collections Trust). These are NOT verbatim spec text and not a substitute for the full spec — they are intended as a procedurally-correct starting point that museums review and refine against their own Collections Trust subscription before claiming Spectrum compliance.

object_entry.name: Spectrum: Object entry
object_entry.description: Document objects arriving on the museum's premises — whether for acquisition, loan, or any other purpose — and assign initial accountability.

steps.name: Receive object
steps.instructions: Receive the object at the agreed point of delivery. Record date, time, conveyor, and method of arrival.

steps.name: Issue receipt
steps.instructions: Issue a written entry receipt to the depositor specifying what has been received and on what terms.

steps.name: Initial condition check
steps.instructions: Conduct a basic condition assessment on arrival — note any damage, packaging issues, or immediate conservation concerns.

steps.name: Provisional location
steps.instructions: Assign a provisional secure location (entry store / triage area). Record the location in the entry record.

steps.name: Handover for processing
steps.instructions: Transfer the object to the appropriate next procedure (Acquisition, Loans in, Cataloguing, etc.). Close the Object entry record once handover is confirmed.

acquisition.name: Spectrum: Acquisition and accessioning
acquisition.description: Take legal and physical ownership of an object and formally enter it into the permanent collection.

steps.name: Acquisition proposal
steps.instructions: Prepare and submit the acquisition proposal — provenance, condition, significance, cost, fit with collecting policy.

steps.name: Governance approval
steps.instructions: Acquisitions panel / trustees consider the proposal against the collecting policy and authorise (or decline) the acquisition.

steps.name: Transfer of title
steps.instructions: Obtain signed transfer-of-title documentation from the previous owner; record terms and conditions.

steps.name: Assign accession number
steps.instructions: Allocate a unique accession number per the institution's numbering scheme and mark the object.

steps.name: Formal accession record
steps.instructions: Create the permanent accession record linking ownership, accession number, and catalogue entry. Notify stakeholders.

inventory.name: Spectrum: Inventory
inventory.description: Verify what the museum holds, where it is, and that records are accurate.

steps.name: Define inventory scope
steps.instructions: Scope the inventory exercise — full collection, sample, or single location. Agree timetable and resources.

steps.name: Physical verification
steps.instructions: Locate and physically check each item within scope. Record what is found and what is missing.

steps.name: Reconcile against records
steps.instructions: Compare findings with catalogue and location records. Identify discrepancies (missing, found-not-recorded, location mismatch).

steps.name: Investigate discrepancies
steps.instructions: Follow up each discrepancy — search known locations, check loans/exits, review recent moves.

steps.name: Inventory report
steps.instructions: Produce the inventory report summarising findings, outstanding items, and recommended follow-up actions.

location_movement.name: Spectrum: Location and movement control
location_movement.description: Track where every object is at all times and document every movement.

steps.name: Move request
steps.instructions: Capture the reason for moving the object — loan, conservation, exhibition, storage reorganisation, etc.

steps.name: Plan move
steps.instructions: Plan route, packing, handlers, and timing. Identify risks and mitigations.

steps.name: Pre-move record
steps.instructions: Capture the current location and the planned destination. Update the location record to 'in transit' as the move starts.

steps.name: Execute move
steps.instructions: Carry out the move under the planned conditions. Note any incidents or deviations.

steps.name: Confirm new location
steps.instructions: Confirm the object is in the new location and update the location record. Close the move.

cataloguing.name: Spectrum: Cataloguing
cataloguing.description: Create the authoritative descriptive record for each object.

steps.name: Identify object
steps.instructions: Establish what the object is — title, classification, materials, technique. Reference authority lists.

steps.name: Descriptive catalogue
steps.instructions: Complete the descriptive fields: dimensions, marks/inscriptions, makers, dates, place of origin, history.

steps.name: Subject indexing
steps.instructions: Assign subject terms, keywords, and any standard-vocabulary references (AAT, ULAN, Iconclass, etc.).

steps.name: Quality review
steps.instructions: Curatorial / senior reviewer checks the catalogue entry for accuracy, completeness, and consistency.

steps.name: Publication
steps.instructions: Make the catalogue record visible internally (and externally where appropriate) once approved.

object_exit.name: Spectrum: Object exit
object_exit.description: Document objects leaving the museum's care, whether temporarily or permanently.

steps.name: Reason for exit
steps.instructions: Record why the object is leaving — loan return, disposal, transfer, conservation off-site, etc.

steps.name: Authorisation
steps.instructions: Obtain the appropriate authorisation per the exit reason (registrar, curator, trustees).

steps.name: Pre-exit record
steps.instructions: Capture final condition photographs and condition report. Update location to 'awaiting exit'.

steps.name: Handover
steps.instructions: Physical handover to the receiving party with signed receipt; record date, time, and carrier.

steps.name: Close record
steps.instructions: Close out the exit record; update the object's status (on loan, deaccessioned, etc.) and notify affected systems.

loans_in.name: Spectrum: Loans in (borrowing)
loans_in.description: Manage objects borrowed from external lenders, from request through return.

steps.name: Loan request
steps.instructions: Prepare the loan request, including purpose, duration, environmental requirements, and security.

steps.name: Lender agreement
steps.instructions: Negotiate and sign the loan agreement; both parties must hold a copy.

steps.name: Insurance & transport
steps.instructions: Arrange insurance (nail-to-nail or as specified) and transport. Confirm couriers if required.

steps.name: Receive & inspect
steps.instructions: Receive the object, inspect against the lender's condition report, record any discrepancies.

steps.name: Return at end of loan
steps.instructions: Return the object to the lender; obtain signed acknowledgement and close the loan record.

loans_out.name: Spectrum: Loans out (lending)
loans_out.description: Manage objects lent from the museum to external borrowers.

steps.name: Borrower request review
steps.instructions: Assess the borrower's request — purpose, venue suitability, security, environmental conditions.

steps.name: Loan agreement
steps.instructions: Issue and sign the loan agreement specifying duration, conditions, insurance, and credit line.

steps.name: Condition documentation
steps.instructions: Photograph and document condition immediately before dispatch. Share with borrower.

steps.name: Dispatch
steps.instructions: Arrange packing, transport, and courier where required. Confirm receipt by borrower.

steps.name: Return inspection
steps.instructions: Inspect on return, compare condition with pre-dispatch documentation, log any change or damage.

insurance.name: Spectrum: Insurance and indemnity
insurance.description: Manage insurance and government indemnity arrangements for the collection.

steps.name: Valuation for insurance
steps.instructions: Establish or refresh insurance values — periodic for permanent collection, per-loan for borrowed/lent items.

steps.name: Policy review
steps.instructions: Review policy coverage against current risks; renew or amend with insurer/indemnity issuer.

steps.name: Document declarations
steps.instructions: Maintain schedules of insured items, exclusions, and declarations required by the insurer.

steps.name: Annual review
steps.instructions: Annual review of cover, values, and claims history.

damage_loss.name: Spectrum: Damage and loss
damage_loss.description: Respond to and document any damage to or loss of collection objects.

steps.name: Discovery & immediate response
steps.instructions: On discovery of damage or loss, secure the area / object, prevent further harm, and notify the registrar.

steps.name: Damage assessment
steps.instructions: Conservator or qualified assessor documents the extent and likely cause of damage.

steps.name: Incident report
steps.instructions: Produce a formal incident report including circumstances, immediate actions, and recommendations.

steps.name: Recovery/repair planning
steps.instructions: Plan treatment, recovery, or — in case of loss — investigation and insurance claim.

steps.name: Final disposition
steps.instructions: Close the incident: restored, written off, or under continuing investigation. Update insurance & catalogue records.

conservation.name: Spectrum: Conservation and collections care
conservation.description: Maintain the physical condition of the collection through preventive care and active conservation treatment.

steps.name: Condition assessment
steps.instructions: Assess the current condition of the object — visual examination, instrumentation if needed.

steps.name: Treatment proposal
steps.instructions: Conservator drafts treatment proposal: aims, methods, materials, risks, alternatives.

steps.name: Approval
steps.instructions: Curator / conservation manager approves the treatment proposal.

steps.name: Treatment & documentation
steps.instructions: Carry out treatment; document materials, techniques, before/after photographs.

steps.name: Preventive care review
steps.instructions: Review the object's storage and display conditions to prevent recurrence; update care plan.

audit.name: Spectrum: Audit
audit.description: Systematically verify the integrity of collection records and procedures.

steps.name: Audit planning
steps.instructions: Define audit scope, sampling method, and timetable. Identify the auditor(s).

steps.name: Sampling & verification
steps.instructions: Carry out the audit checks — physical verification, record cross-checks, procedure compliance.

steps.name: Findings documentation
steps.instructions: Document findings in detail — what was checked, what was found, what was missing or anomalous.

steps.name: Management response
steps.instructions: Management reviews findings and prepares responses / corrective action plan.

steps.name: Audit report
steps.instructions: Final audit report shared with trustees / governance, with agreed actions tracked.

condition_check.name: Spectrum: Object condition checking and technical assessment
condition_check.description: Detailed assessment of an object's physical state, periodically and at key moments (loans in/out, exhibition).

steps.name: Initial visual inspection
steps.instructions: Visual inspection covering structural, surface, and material condition.

steps.name: Detailed examination
steps.instructions: Where required, detailed examination including magnification, lighting variation, photography.

steps.name: Technical analysis
steps.instructions: Optional: technical analysis (X-ray, UV, infrared, material analysis) where indicated and resourced.

steps.name: Condition report
steps.instructions: Produce the condition report with photographs, observations, and any recommendations.

valuation.name: Spectrum: Object valuation
valuation.description: Establish and maintain insurance, fair-market, and replacement values for collection objects.

steps.name: Valuation request
steps.instructions: Capture the trigger for valuation — insurance, loan, deaccession, periodic review.

steps.name: Comparable research
steps.instructions: Research recent sales / valuations of comparable objects. Document sources.

steps.name: Independent appraisal
steps.instructions: Where required, commission an independent appraiser. Confirm their credentials.

steps.name: Documentation & review schedule
steps.instructions: Record the valuation, basis, valuer, date, and schedule next review.

risk_management.name: Spectrum: Risk management
risk_management.description: Systematically identify, assess, and mitigate risks to the collection.

steps.name: Risk identification
steps.instructions: Identify risks across storage, display, transit, environment, security, governance.

steps.name: Risk assessment & scoring
steps.instructions: Assess likelihood and impact of each risk; assign a score and priority.

steps.name: Mitigation planning
steps.instructions: Plan mitigation actions — preventive, detective, corrective. Assign owners and deadlines.

steps.name: Implementation
steps.instructions: Carry out the agreed mitigations. Document evidence.

steps.name: Periodic review
steps.instructions: Periodic review of the risk register; revise scoring, add new risks, retire mitigated ones.

emergency_planning.name: Spectrum: Emergency planning for collections
emergency_planning.description: Prepare for and rehearse the museum's response to fire, flood, theft, conflict, or other emergencies.

steps.name: Hazard identification
steps.instructions: Identify the hazards relevant to the building and collection (fire, flood, theft, civil disturbance, etc.).

steps.name: Plan development
steps.instructions: Develop response plans — salvage priorities, evacuation order, emergency contacts, equipment cache.

steps.name: Staff training
steps.instructions: Train all relevant staff in the plan, their roles, and use of equipment.

steps.name: Drill & exercise
steps.instructions: Run scheduled drills to test the plan; capture lessons learned.

steps.name: Plan review/update
steps.instructions: Update the plan based on exercise outcomes, staff changes, and new risks.

use_of_collections.name: Spectrum: Use of collections
use_of_collections.description: Document and manage authorised access to and use of collection objects (research, education, exhibition).

steps.name: Use request
steps.instructions: Capture the user's request — who, what, why, when, how (handling? imaging?).

steps.name: Eligibility review
steps.instructions: Verify identity, purpose, and any ethical / rights considerations.

steps.name: Approval & conditions
steps.instructions: Approve (or decline) with specific conditions — supervision, copying restrictions, photography.

steps.name: Supervised use
steps.instructions: Facilitate the access under the agreed conditions; record what was used.

steps.name: Post-use condition check
steps.instructions: Check the object's condition after use; document any change. Close the access record.

rights_management.name: Spectrum: Rights management
rights_management.description: Document and manage rights (copyright, moral rights, traditional knowledge, etc.) attached to collection objects.

steps.name: Rights identification
steps.instructions: Identify the rights that apply — copyright, moral rights, performers' rights, indigenous TK, contractual restrictions.

steps.name: Rights documentation
steps.instructions: Record rightsholders, terms, expiry dates, and supporting documentation.

steps.name: Permission requests
steps.instructions: Where the museum needs permission to use an object (e.g. image online), request and record permissions.

steps.name: Licence tracking
steps.instructions: Maintain a current view of in-force licences and their conditions.

steps.name: Periodic review
steps.instructions: Periodic review — rights expiring, new claims, policy changes.

reproduction.name: Spectrum: Reproduction
reproduction.description: Manage the creation of copies and surrogates of collection objects (digitisation, replicas, publishable images).

steps.name: Reproduction request
steps.instructions: Capture the request — what object, what format, what purpose.

steps.name: Rights clearance
steps.instructions: Verify the museum has the right to reproduce; obtain external permission if needed.

steps.name: Production
steps.instructions: Produce the reproduction — digitisation shoot, replica fabrication, etc. Document parameters.

steps.name: Quality control
steps.instructions: QC the output — colour, resolution, metadata, naming.

steps.name: Delivery & record
steps.instructions: Deliver to the requester and store the master in the digital asset system or replica register.

deaccessioning.name: Spectrum: Deaccessioning and disposal
deaccessioning.description: Formally remove an object from the permanent collection through governance-approved disposal.

steps.name: Deaccession proposal
steps.instructions: Prepare the deaccession case — rationale, due diligence, ethical considerations, disposal method.

steps.name: Governance review
steps.instructions: Collections committee and trustees consider the case; approve, decline, or refer.

steps.name: Authorisation
steps.instructions: Final authorisation from governing body; record decision and any conditions.

steps.name: Disposal execution
steps.instructions: Execute the disposal — transfer to another institution, return to donor, sale, or last-resort destruction.

steps.name: Permanent record
steps.instructions: Record the deaccession permanently — even after disposal, the museum keeps the documentation indefinitely.

retrospective_doc.name: Spectrum: Retrospective documentation
retrospective_doc.description: Catch up on documentation for historic items where records are incomplete or non-existent.

steps.name: Identify undocumented items
steps.instructions: Identify items needing retrospective documentation — survey existing records, flag gaps.

steps.name: Prioritise
steps.instructions: Prioritise by significance, risk, and resourcing constraints.

steps.name: Catalogue
steps.instructions: Catalogue each prioritised item to current standards; reuse existing legacy data where reliable.

steps.name: Verify
steps.instructions: Cross-check identification and provenance against external sources where possible.

steps.name: Integrate
steps.instructions: Integrate the new catalogue records into the main collection system; close the retrospective project for that batch.
